project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Page Layout Redesign
overview: Redesign the browse page layout so the header and search bar are always visible. The page splits into two independent scroll containers: a fixed-height sidebar and a main content area. Neither scrolls the page — only their own content.
goals: Header (logo + Upload button) fixed at top of viewport
Search bar fixed directly below the header — always visible, prominent
Sidebar and main grid each scroll independently within the remaining viewport height
No full-page scroll — the viewport itself never scrolls
nonGoals: No changes to search logic or API
No changes to filter sidebar content or filter components
successMetrics: Scrolling the asset grid does not move the header or search bar
Scrolling the asset grid does not scroll the sidebar
Scrolling the sidebar filters does not scroll the asset grid
The viewport body never has a scrollbar
uiNotes: Layout hierarchy: <html>/<body> height:100vh overflow:hidden → App wrapper flex column height:100vh → Header (fixed height ~48px) → SearchBar row (fixed height ~56px, padding) → Body row (flex:1, overflow:hidden) → Sidebar (width:240px, overflow-y:auto, height:100%) + Main (flex:1, overflow-y:auto, height:100%)
Header: navy background (#001B6B), Royal Caribbean logo left, Upload button right, height 48px, padding 0 24px
Search bar row: white background, border-bottom 1px solid #e5e7eb, padding 12px 24px, contains the SearchBar component full width
Sidebar: border-right 1px solid #e5e7eb, padding 16px, overflow-y auto
Main content area: padding 16px 24px, overflow-y auto — contains results count text + asset grid
No changes to the visual appearance of any individual component — only the container layout changes
rules: App.jsx or BrowsePage.jsx must set body/html to overflow:hidden via useEffect on mount (cleanup on unmount)
The three-row column layout (header, search, body) must use flexbox with the body row taking flex:1 and overflow:hidden
Sidebar and main must each have height:100% and overflow-y:auto so they scroll independently
ResultsCount line ('Showing X of Y assets') stays inside the main scroll area, above the grid

stories.title: Fixed header + search bar layout shell
stories.description: As a user, I want the header and search bar to always be visible so I can search without scrolling back to the top.
acceptanceCriteria: App.jsx renders a flex-column wrapper that fills 100vh with overflow:hidden
Header row is 48px tall, navy background, contains logo (left) and Upload button (right)
Search bar row is below header, white background with bottom border, contains SearchBar component at full width
Body row below search takes flex:1, overflow:hidden, and contains sidebar + main in a flex row
body/html overflow:hidden is set on mount and cleaned up on unmount
Vitest test: BrowsePage renders a header element and a search input
Vitest test: sidebar and main content area both exist in the rendered output

stories.title: Independent scroll containers for sidebar and grid
stories.description: As a user, I want the filter sidebar and asset grid to scroll independently so filters stay accessible while I browse.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar div has overflow-y:auto, height:100%, fixed width 240px
Main content div has overflow-y:auto, height:100%, flex:1
Scrolling the grid does not affect the sidebar scroll position
Scrolling the sidebar does not affect the grid scroll position
Results count text ('Showing X of Y assets') is inside the main scroll container, above the grid
No scrollbar appears on the body/html element
Vitest test: layout renders without body-level overflow
